D9 Biology L4 Analyze Reference

Bird Tetrachromacy — A 4th UV Cone

birds have four cone types — UVS (~370 nm), SWS (~440), MWS (~510), LWS (~570) — letting them perceive a colour dimension we cannot

Most diurnal birds have four cone classes plus oil droplets that sharpen the spectral bands. The 4th class — UVS — peaks at ~370 nm UV. Tetrachromatic colour space is 3D for us, 4D for birds. Many bird species look monochrome to us but display striking UV patterns to mates.

Toggle "human view" vs "bird UV view" of the same blue tit — the head crown's reflectance suddenly stands out under UV.
